titleStmt.title: Letter from Thomas J. O'Connell to Robert Chalmers, 13 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Thomas J. O'Connell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
This is a letter from Thomas J. O'Connell (b.1885), secretary for the Tralee and Dingle Railway Company, to Sir. Robert Chalmers (1858-1938). Chalmers was sent to Ireland as under-secretary in the aftermath of the Easter Rising of 1916. He replaced Sir Matthew Nathan who had resigned along with Augustine Birrell, his chief secretary, following the insurrection.
The letter concerns an amendment to the Tralee and Dingle (committee management), order 1896. O'Connell wishes to be informed when the order becomes operative.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/5613/8180/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Tralee and Dingle Railway Company
Secretary's Office
Tralee 13th May, 1916.
G.7/220.
30. Sir :- With further reference to your letter (4339 - 6th ult.)
and mine of 10th ult. re amendment of Tralee & Dingle (Committee
of Management) Order 1896 will you kindly let me know when the new
order becomes operative and oblige. I am, SIR,
Your obedient servant, T. J O'Connell
Secretary. The Under-Secretary,
Dublin Castle,
D U B L I N.
